title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Gaelic origin
description: Highland Scotland — 'uisge beatha' (water of life). Earliest documented distilling records 1494 Exchequer Rolls: 'To friar John Cor, eight bolls of malt...to make aqua vitae'.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Gaelic origin
description: Ireland — competing origin claim. 'Uisce beatha' same meaning. Pot-still Irish tradition.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Scots-Irish migration to America
description: Ulster — Scots-Irish emigration 18th century carries distilling to America.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Scots-Irish migration to America
description: Pittsburgh PA — Whiskey Rebellion 1791–1794. Washington leads federal army against western farmers resisting distillation tax.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Scots-Irish migration to America
description: Louisville / Bardstown KY — Bourbon heartland. Limestone water + corn + new charred oak casks create Bourbon character.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Scots-Irish migration to America
description: Lynchburg TN — Jack Daniel's Tennessee whiskey. Nearest Green (enslaved Black distiller) teaches Jack Daniel the charcoal mellowing process.

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Japanese whiskey lineage
description: Tokyo — Masataka Taketsuru studies Scottish whisky 1918–1920, returns to build Yamazaki (1923) + Nikka (1934).

title: LAYER 1 — Historical Trade: Japanese whiskey lineage
description: Yamazaki (Kyoto prefecture) — Japan's first malt whisky distillery (Suntory).

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: uisge beatha → whiskey
description: Scots Gaelic — 'uisge beatha' literally 'water of life'.

title: LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: uisge beatha → whiskey
LAYER 2 — Language Diffusion: uisge beatha → whiskey
description: English — 'whiskey' / 'whisky'. Scottish + Japanese + Canadian spell without 'e'; Irish + American spell with 'e'.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Scotland — 135+ active distilleries. Scotch exports £6B/year, 10,000+ jobs.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Kentucky — 95% of world Bourbon. $9B industry, 22,000 jobs.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Japan — premium world-class whisky. Yamazaki 50-year bottle set auction record $795,000 in 2020.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: Canada — rye whisky tradition; Crown Royal is global.

title: LAYER 3 — Modern Supply Chain (today)
description: India — world's largest whisky market by volume (though 'Indian Malt Whisky' often contains cheaper spirit bases).
